allies.rationale_text: Resilience gives internal-locus-of-control a practical recovery frame by keeping focus on controllable responses to setbacks.
allies.source_quote: Resilience is strongly linked to this internal focus

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps internal-locus-of-control distinguish between agency and the system structure that must be redesigned.
allies.source_quote: viewing the organization as a machine

structured_tensions.tension_text: Internal-locus-of-control conflicts with status-quo-bias when agency is ceded to default environmental inertia.
Internal-locus-of-control conflicts with commitment-bias when prior identity commitments block corrective action.
Internal-locus-of-control conflicts with confirmation-bias when feedback is processed to protect the sense of control instead of revising it.
